### Post by _sluimers_ on 2007-11-08
My Cdrom won't find my CD's nor DVD's

cat /etc/fstab gives me:

```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config --
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda3 :
UUID=1d21b8ed-6eb6-42c1-b775-2d14a4259f15 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda4 :
UUID=857cfec2-8182-4bc4-9319-e9554d7a110a none swap sw 0 0
/dev/dvd /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0

```

I never understood the whole mounting thing,
so here's what I tried:

```

rogier@rogier-laptop:/dev$ sudo mount /dev/dvd /media/cdrom0
mount: block device /dev/scd0 is write-protected, mounting read-only
mount: you must specify the filesystem type

```

---

### Post by taurus on 2007-11-08
Try

```
sudo mount -t iso9660 /dev/dvd /media/cdrom0
```
What kind of CD/DVD is it you're trying to mount?

---

### Post by TeaSwigger on 2007-11-08
Hm it might actually be 

/dev/scd0

You could try replacing

/dev/dvd /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0

with 

/dev/scd0       /media/cdrom0   udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec 0       0

By all means make a backup of the file first! And jot down the name. Then reboot. It's always good practice in case for some reason it doesn't like the change; then you can just boot into recovery mode and put the safety copy back.

If the drive doesn't show at /media/cdrom0, check that /media/cdrom0 is linked to /dev/scd0 in this example.

---
